raw_output: {"predictions":[{"common_name":"Downy Woodpecker","scientific_name":"Dryobates pubescens","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.95,"field_marks":"Small black-and-white woodpecker with spotted wings, white back, black head with white stripe, male has red nape patch (not visible here); barred flight feathers and tail; compact size and wing shape match"},{"other_common_names":["Hairy Woodpecker"],"scientific_name":"Dryobates villosus","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.3,"field_marks":"Larger, longer bill, less distinct white spots on wings, but similar plumage; size and bill length not clearly discernible in flight"},{"common_name":"Northern Flicker","scientific_name":"Colaptes auratus","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.1,"field_marks":"Usually shows brownish coloration, black spots on belly, yellow or red underwing; this bird lacks those features"},{"common_name":"Pileated Woodpecker","scientific_name":"Dryocopus pileatus","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.05,"field_marks":"Much larger, prominent red crest, solid black back; not matching size or pattern"},{"common_name":"Woodpecker sp.","scientific_name":null,"rank_hint":"family","confidence":0.02,"field_marks":"Black-and-white plumage, zygodactyl foot posture inferred from flight posture, typical Picidae wing shape"}],"abstain":false,"abstain_reason":null,"overall_confidence":0.92}
